votes.reason: The Python code correctly checks the lengths of the lists and verifies that each element in the result list is the absolute difference of the corresponding elements in the game and guess lists, exactly matching the Coq specification.

The Python code checks the same three conditions as the Coq spec: (1) length of game equals length of guess, (2) length of result equals length of game, and (3) for each index i, result[i] equals the absolute difference of game[i] and guess[i]. Both return/hold true exactly when all conditions are satisfied for concrete inputs.
